LAW AND MOTION 301, STATUS CONFERENCE CONTINUED FROM OCT-09-2002 TO LAW AND MOTION AT OCT-23-2002, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301. APPEARANCES MADE. THIS MATTER WAS HEARD WITH LINE 22, 603217 (KATHY LIU VS. WILLIAM PRYOR). MR. CURTICE PROVIDED COPIES OF DECLARATIONS FOR THE COURT'S REVIEW. MATTER PASSED AND RECALLED AFTER A SHORT RECESS TO REVIEW SAID DOCUMENTS. STATUS CONFERENCE HELD. THE COURT MAINTAINED THE STAY OF THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION (603217) AND ORDERED ANOTHER STATUS CONFERENCE ON THE STAY TO TAKE PLACE ON OCTOBER 23, 2002. THE ISSUE OF AN UNDERTAKING MAY BE ADDRESSED ON OCTOBER 23RD. MR. BORNSTEIN TO FILE PAPERS BY OCTOBER 15TH; MR. CURTICE TO REPLY THERETO BY OCTOBER 21ST. BOTH PARTIES TO SERVE BY FAX TRANSMISSION OR BY PERSONAL SERVICE. COURTESY COPIES TO BE SUPPLIED TO THE COURT. ALSO, IF MR. CURTICE IS TO BRING A MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE, THEN HE MUST DO SO AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. HON. JAMES J. MCBRIDE (301/DR) ERIC THRONE, CSR 7855

Status Conference
Off Calendar Nov-19-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lawrence L. Curtice, Esq., 543-5805, for Ella Pryor in Actions #412866 and #603217
Jonathan H. Bornstein, Esq., BORNSTEIN & BORNSTEIN 409-7611, for Kathy Liu in Actions #412866 and #603217
Status Conference held, counsel stipulated to allow the amended complaint filed in the unlimited jurisdiction case, the answer on file deemed as answer. Status Conference
Off Calendar Oct-09-2002 Continued To Oct-23-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Lawrence L. Curtice, Esq. 543-5805 for Plaintiff
Jonathan H. Bornstein, Esq. 409-7611 for Defendant KATHY LIU
* * * This matter was heard with Line 22, 603217 (KATHY LIU vs. WILLIAM PRYOR).
Mr. Curtice provided copies of Declarations for the Court's review. Matter passed and recalled after a short recess to review said documents.
Status conference held. The Court maintained the stay of the Unlawful Detainer action (603217) and ordered another status conference on the stay to take place on OCTOBER 23, 2002. The issue of an undertaking may be addressed on October 23rd. Mr. Bornstein to file papers by October 15th; Mr. Curtice to reply thereto by October 21st. Both parties to serve by FAX transmission or by personal service. Courtesy copies to be supplied to the Court. Also, if Mr. Curtice is to bring a motion to consolidate, then he must do so as soon as possible. Hon. James J. McBride (301/dr) Eric Throne, CSR 7855
